title: To our colleagues in the healthcare community:

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
To our colleagues in the healthcare community:

We know that 2020 had its fair share of challenges. Despite these challenges, you have courageously prioritized the health of your members and patients. We are grateful and honored to have served so many of you as your trusted partner this past year.

As 2021 begins, our country’s healthcare system faces a year of rebuilding and moving forward into a new future. The norms in our industry have changed significantly with the push toward adoption of new technology to drive better communication and efficiency. MCG Health looks forward to this more advanced healthcare ecosystem.  We are well-positioned to help address your clinical and business needs with our proven industry-leading guidelines and software solutions coupled with advanced technology.  Through connected, interoperable, smart systems, MCG will help our customers deliver effective care while reducing administrative burden.

By now, you’ve heard the recent news that UnitedHealth Group’s Optum will acquire Change Healthcare and their InterQual Guidelines. MCG wants to assure you of our ongoing commitment to our customers across the healthcare continuum.

Unlike Change Healthcare, MCG Health has always been a private company. We intend to remain private under Hearst Health in order to provide the most patient-centered, unbiased, evidence-based guidelines in the market, without the influence of specialty societies, regulatory bodies, customers, or special interests. Our mission is to drive optimal outcomes for healthcare consumers. MCG is well-resourced and on a strong trajectory of growth.  We promise to continue delivering the best customer service for our esteemed clients and partners and are committed to our customers’ success.

MCG is proud of our independence and the rigor of our content development process that, for decades, has provided the most patient-centric, outcome-oriented guidelines on the market.  With the addition of investments made in data science, machine learning and artificial intelligence, we are excited about the future.

As you have come to expect from MCG, we will communicate openly to address any questions you may have regarding this industry news or any MCG matter. Please reach out to us if you’d like to discuss any issues or concerns.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Thank you for your ongoing dedication to healthcare and for trusting MCG Health as your partner. We are committed to supporting you as we move into a new era of healthcare together.

Sincerely,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

CEO, MCG Health

turns.text:
1. MCG Health acknowledges the challenges healthcare professionals faced in 2020 and expresses gratitude for serving as a trusted partner.

2. MCG Health is well-positioned to address clinical and business needs with advanced technology and promises to continue delivering the best customer service.

3. MCG Health will remain a private company and promises to provide unbiased, evidence-based guidelines while using investments in data science, machine learning, and artificial intelligence to drive optimal outcomes for healthcare consumers.

turns.text: Act like you're a consultant. You're consulting MCG Health a Seattle based HealthTech company. Learn as much as you can about their growth strategy for the next few years and spit out 5 bullets each of Long-Term growth initiatives and Short-Term growth initiatives.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
As an AI language model, I don't have access to MCG Health's internal documents or plans. However, based on publicly available information, here are some possible long-term and short-term growth initiatives for MCG Health: 

Long-term growth initiatives:
- Expand their data science and machine learning capabilities by leveraging AI to enhance their existing guidelines and decision-making tools.
- Develop new products and solutions that leverage advanced technologies to deliver more personalized and effective care to patients, such as telehealth or remote monitoring systems.
- Build partnerships with other healthtech companies or healthcare providers to access new markets or expand their services to patients and clients.
- Expand their market presence by targeting new geographies or sectors, such as international markets, government agencies, or payer organizations.
- Increase their investments in research and development to stay at the forefront of the latest trends and innovations in the healthcare industry.

Short-term growth initiatives:
- Develop marketing campaigns and sales initiatives to promote their existing products and services to new customers or clients.
- Enhance their customer service and support functions to improve customer satisfaction and retention.
- Strengthen their partnerships with key industry players, such as insurers or hospital systems, to secure new business opportunities or partnerships.
- Optimize their operations and processes to reduce costs and increase efficiency, such as by implementing automation or outsourcing certain functions.
- Streamline their product portfolio and focus on their core competencies to ensure they are delivering maximum value to their customers while maintaining profitability.
